rulings.Case Title: CANON INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC. VS. REESHA CAPITAL , LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay For Arbitration And Restore Case To Active Docket; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Daniel A. Oberle
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 21, 2025 Line 1. PLAINTIFF CANON INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC.'s Motion To Lift Stay For Arbitration And Restore Case To Active Docket. Plaintiff Canon Investment Group, LLC's unopposed Motion To Lift Stay For Arbitration And Restore Case To Active Docket is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the stay is lifted and the action is returned to the active docket. The case focuses on a contract for the sale of real property and, thus, it is governed by San Francisco Superior Court Local Rule 8.10. The parties are ordered to review the Local Rues to ensure filings are made in, and motions are noticed for, the correct department. Motions not noticed for the correct department may be ordered off calendar. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: 822 W WASHINGTON LP DBA NUEVA VENICE, VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Voluntary Dismissal Of Class Action Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 3.770
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 21, 2025 Line 2. PLAINTIFF 822 W WASHINGTON LP DBA NUEVA VENICE's Motion For Voluntary Dismissal Of Class Action Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 3.770. Plaintiff 822 W Washington's Motion For Voluntary Dismissal Of Class Action Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 3.770 is CONTINUED TO June 3, 2025, at 9:00 am in this Department. No later than May 19, 2025, Moving Party shall a supplemental brief and any supporting declarations re the below issues. Any opposition must be filed no later than May 26, 2025. Courtesy copies of all such filings must be provided to this department within two court days of filing. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: ST. PAULUS EVANGELICAL LUTHERAN CHURCH, A VS. MERKER ARCHITECTURAL GROUP INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Protective Order To Exclude Robert Kent Clay From Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 21, 2025 Line 3. PLAINTIFF ST. PAULUS EVANGELICAL LUTHERAN CHURCH's Motion For Protective Order To Exclude Robert Kent Clay From Depositions. Plaintiff St. Paulus Evangelical Lutheran Church's motion to compel further deposition of Defendant Marc Dana Merker and the Person Most Knowledgeable of Defendant Merker Architectural Group, Inc. and for a protective order excluding Counsel Robert Kent Clay from future depositions is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. rulings.Case Title: YALING CHEN VS. RICHARD HUM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, April 21, 2025 Line 7. 1 - DEFENDANTs ANN HUM ,and EDMUND HUM, BELINDA HUM's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off calendar as untimely per CCP 1005. Additionally, movant failed to provide courtesy copies, as required by SFLR 2.7B, and the Alvarez declaration fails to show parties met and conferred in person, by telephone, or by video, regarding the issues raised in this motion to strike per CCP 435.5. The response to the complaint is now due on May 21, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
